question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted sidecar file whose `TransactionSidecarRecord.getConsensusTimestamp()` collides with a legitimate transaction's timestamp, causing `recordItem.setSidecarRecords()` to overwrite the real sidecar data with attacker-controlled records, thereby corrupting the mirror node's view of submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file where `sidecar.getHash()` and `sidecar.getActualHash()` are both null, causing `Arrays.equals(null, null)` to return true and bypassing the hash integrity check, allowing tampered sidecar transaction records to be accepted without detection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file with an empty byte array for both `sidecar.getHash()` and `sidecar.getActualHash()`, so that `Arrays.equals()` returns true on two empty arrays, bypassing hash verification and allowing tampered transaction sidecar records to be persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `acceptedTypes.isEmpty()` shortcut in the sidecar type filter so that when `sidecarProperties.getTypeOrdinals()` returns an empty set, all sidecar types including attacker-injected unknown types pass the filter, causing tampered transaction records to be associated with legitimate `RecordItem` objects?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSidecarType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a `TransactionSidecarRecord` with an unrecognized `getSidecarRecordsCase()` value, causing `getSidecarType()` to call `Utility.handleRecoverableError()` and yield `SIDECAR_TYPE_UNKNOWN_VALUE`, which then passes the `acceptedTypes.isEmpty()` filter and injects the tampered record into a legitimate transaction's sidecar list?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the `version` field of a `RecordFile` to be less than `ProtoRecordFileReader.VERSION`, causing `setStreamFileIndex()` to invoke `super.setStreamFileIndex()` and assign an incorrect sequential index, breaking the block ordering of submitted transactions in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cutoverService.get()` callback mechanism so that a race condition between `cutoverService.getLastRecordFile()` and `lastStreamFile.set()` causes the downloader to re-process an already-processed record file, leading to duplicate transaction records being injected into the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `RecordFile` whose `getSidecars()` list contains a sidecar with a name that, when passed to `StreamFilename.from(recordFilename, sidecar.getName())`, resolves to a path-traversal location, causing `getSidecar()` to fetch an attacker-controlled file from outside the expected bucket path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file containing multiple `TransactionSidecarRecord` entries all sharing the same `consensusTimestamp`, causing the `Multimaps.toMultimap()` collector to accumulate all of them under one key, and then `recordItem.setSidecarRecords()` to associate all attacker-controlled records with a single legitimate transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a sidecar file where `sidecarFileReader.read()` populates `sidecar.getActualHash()` with a value computed from attacker-controlled bytes, and simultaneously the expected `sidecar.getHash()` is sourced from an unverified field in the parent `RecordFile`, allowing a fully crafted sidecar to pass hash verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `downloadSidecars()` is called inside `onVerified()` before `super.onVerified()` persists the record file, so that a failure in sidecar hash verification throws `HashMismatchException` and prevents the legitimate record file from being notified, effectively causing a denial-of-service on transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar filename (via `sidecar.getName()`) containing special characters or path separators that cause `StreamFilename.from(recordFilename, sidecar.getName())` to construct a `bucketFilePath` pointing to an arbitrary cloud storage location, fetching attacker-controlled transaction sidecar data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `SidecarFile` entry into a `RecordFile`'s sidecar list with a `types` list that matches `acceptedTypes`, but whose actual `TransactionSidecarRecord` contents contain tampered contract state changes, causing the mirror node to record false contract state for submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive `Mono` pipeline in `getSidecar()` such that a `streamFileProvider.get()` call returns a cached or stale `StreamFileData` object, allowing previously tampered sidecar bytes to be re-read and re-associated with new transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the `.block()` call in `downloadSidecars()` to return a `records` multimap containing entries with timestamps that do not correspond to any `RecordItem`, so that the unmatched sidecar records are silently dropped, hiding evidence of tampered transactions from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file with a valid hash but tampered `TransactionSidecarRecord` contents where the `consensusTimestamp` has been altered to match a different legitimate transaction, causing `recordItem.setSidecarRecords()` to associate the tampered sidecar with the wrong transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Flux.fromIterable(recordFile.getSidecars()).flatMap()` pipeline to inject a sidecar with a very large number of `TransactionSidecarRecord` entries, causing the `.block()` call to consume excessive memory and crash the importer, resulting in a denial-of-service that halts transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSidecarType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionSidecarRecord` with `getSidecarRecordsCase()` returning `DEFAULT_INSTANCE`, causing the `default` branch in `getSidecarType()` to log the consensus timestamp and sidecar case via `Utility.handleRecoverableError()`, leaking internal transaction metadata through log output?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a `RecordFile` to include a `getSidecars()` list that is non-empty but contains entries with `getTypes()` returning an empty list, bypassing the `sidecar.getTypes().stream().anyMatch(acceptedTypes::contains)` filter and causing unfiltered sidecar data to be downloaded and associated with submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user forge a `RecordFile` with `getVersion()` returning exactly `ProtoRecordFileReader.VERSION - 1`, causing `setStreamFileIndex()` to call `super.setStreamFileIndex()` which assigns `lastIndex + 1`, and if `lastStreamFile` is empty, assigns index 0, allowing the attacker to reset the block index and corrupt transaction ordering?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file whose `SidecarFile.getRecords()` returns a list containing null entries, causing a `NullPointerException` in `TransactionSidecarRecord::getConsensusTimestamp` during the `Multimaps.toMultimap()` collection step, crashing the reactive pipeline and preventing legitimate transactions from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sidecar file where `sidecarFileReader.read()` sets `sidecar.getActualHash()` to a zero-length byte array and `sidecar.getHash()` is also zero-length, making `Arrays.equals()` return true, so that a completely empty/tampered sidecar passes hash verification and its records are injected into transaction items?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `flatMapIterable(SidecarFile::getRecords)` step to inject a `SidecarFile` whose `getRecords()` returns an extremely large list, causing unbounded memory growth in the `ArrayListMultimap` collected by `.block()`, leading to an out-of-memory condition that disrupts transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar file where `sidecar.getName()` is a URL-encoded path that, after `StreamFilename.from()` parsing, resolves to a different node's directory, causing the downloader to fetch a sidecar from an unintended node and associate its transaction records with the current record file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the `RecordFile.getSidecars()` list to include a `SidecarFile` with a `name` that matches an existing legitimate sidecar filename but with a different `index`, causing `getSidecar()` to fetch the wrong sidecar file and associate incorrect transaction records with the record file?
